expectations.text: Response explicitly recommends NOT clipping or patching the negative values and proceeding
expectations.evidence: First sentence: 'Do not clip the values to zero and move on. Negative travel times are a hard sanity-check failure.'

expectations.text: Response mentions git reset --hard HEAD or equivalent rollback command
expectations.evidence: Step 1: 'git reset --hard HEAD' shown in code block

expectations.text: Response includes or references the Issue_Report.md schema (Issue, Timestamp, Failure Point, Expected Bound, Actual Output, Action Taken)
expectations.evidence: Step 2 shows full YAML schema: Issue, Timestamp, Failure Point, Expected Bound, Actual Output, Action Taken — all 6 fields present

expectations.text: Response identifies this as a sanity check failure requiring root cause investigation, not a quick fix
expectations.evidence: 'Investigate the root cause before rerunning' with 4 specific root cause categories: coordinate/unit mismatch, formula sign error, pandas-numpy boundary bug, index misalignment
